key_insight: Pathway-informed autoencoders can integrate multi-omic cancer data while keeping the learned representations biologically interpretable and useful for prediction.
clinical_relevance: The framework may help stratify breast cancer patients by risk and subtype using multi-omic signals that map more directly to pathways relevant for clinical interpretation.
method: Built pathway-activity autoencoders with biologically constrained architecture to embed prior pathway knowledge.
Integrated multiple omics layers for breast cancer analysis, including gene, protein, and microRNA expression.
Compared performance for survival prediction and subtype classification, while examining the contribution of each omics layer and model robustness.
evidence_signal: In breast cancer evaluations, multi-omic integration improved end-task performance, with gene, protein, and microRNA layers contributing most strongly according to the abstract.
watch_item: Excessive regularisation can reduce predictive performance, so model tuning is important to balance robustness with accuracy.
paper_title: Biologically Informed Deep Neural Networks for Multi-Omic Integration, Pathway Activity Inference and Risk Stratification in Cancer
